claim_text:
A recent vulnerability in Google Chrome's Gemini AI (CVE-2026-0628) allowed malicious extensions to:
1. Hijack the AI assistant.
2. Access your webcam and microphone.
3. Take screenshots of your PC.
4. Steal local files.
All through a "harmless" browser extension you installed.
notes: TikTok overlay cites a ZDNet security article in the screenshot.

claim_text: OpenAI was caught deleting evidence that they trained on books. A US court (2025) ordered them to prove they didn't use illegal material. If guilty: $150,000 fine per work.
notes: The screenshot includes a Bloomberg Tax headline and timestamp.

claim_text:
OpenAI Said 1 Year. The Code Says 3 Years.
Persona's source code leak revealed:
1. User biometric data retention is set to 1095 days (3 years).
2. OpenAI publicly claims only 1 year.
3. If you're rejected, no explanation, no appeal, but your data stays.
notes: The image shows code and file-tree screenshots as the cited source.

claim_text:
In the dark web hackers discovered OpenAI's secret "watchlist" system with Persona (2026):
1. 269 checks on every user.
2. Your selfie is compared to global terrorists, sanctions lists, and political figures.
3. A "suspicious entity detection" AI judges your face, with no appeal.
4. Built to report directly to U.S. FinCEN.
source_shown: No external source shown; TikTok @neonash78 overlay
notes: No external publication title is visible on this slide.

claim_text:
Yes, for YOUR privacy:
1. No cloud = No spying.
2. No company = No data selling.
3. No internet = No government requests.
But No, for the BIG problems:
1. Stolen movies? The model was already trained on them.
2. Killer robots? Governments run them locally too.
3. RAM prices? Local AI needs expensive hardware too.
4. Environmental damage? Training already happened.
source_shown: No external source shown; TikTok @neonash78 overlay
notes: This slide presents a split claim about local AI.

claim_text:
AI Trained on Copyrighted Content, And They Know It
In December 2025, Italian media giant Mediaset sued Perplexity AI:
1. The AI used thousands of copyrighted films and shows for training.
2. No permission. No payment.
Just theft.
3. The case is ongoing.
source_shown: CMS publication: Italy - RTI S.p.A and Medusa Film S.p.A. v Perplexity AI Inc.
notes: The screenshot includes a CMS law article reference to the Italian case.

claim_text:
Disney sent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance's "Seedance 2.0" (February 2026):
1. The AI generated Spider-Man, Star Wars, Marvel characters without permission.
2. Disney called it a "smash-and-grab" theft of their intellectual property.
3. The Motion Picture Association condemned it.
notes: The slide cites a visible article snippet but the publication name is not legible in English.

claim_text:
Hackers from "Department of Peace" leaked DHS documents (March 2026):
1. AI tools analyzing 911 calls to predict crime patterns.
2. Automated surveillance at airports tracking passengers in real-time.
3. Phone attachments allowing agents to collect biometric data on the spot.
4. Contracts with Palantir, Oracle, Raytheon, Microsoft.
notes: The overlay goes beyond the visible Mirror US headline.

claim_text:
US Department of Defense Directive 3000.09 provides a legal path for fully autonomous weapons. Meaning:
1. AI can select targets.
2. AI can engage targets.
3. "Human oversight" is often just a button-pusher.
notes: The screenshot cites an Anadolu Agency article about Pentagon AI contracts and surveillance debate.

claim_text:
OpenAI signed a contract with the US Department of Defense.
Their models run on classified networks. They claim "no mass surveillance" and "human oversight", but legal experts say these terms are loosely defined and can change.
One company (Anthropic) rejected the Pentagon's terms.
OpenAI accepted.
notes: This slide uses the same Anadolu Agency article as a source snippet.

claim_text:
Academic research reveals:
1. China and the US are racing to build autonomous weapons.
2. China's AI can simulate 10,000 battle scenarios in 48 seconds.
3. The US is integrating AI into nuclear command and control.
4. No international laws regulate AI weapons effectively.
notes: The screenshot shows a journal article about General Artificial Intelligence and the US-PRC arms race.

claim_text:
The Numbers (March 2026):
1. 51% of companies will lay off workers in 2026 because of AI.
2. 47% will eliminate entry-level roles by 2027.
CEO: Jack Dorsey (Block): "AI changes what it means to build a company."
4,000 laid off.
CEO: Zubin Appoo (WiseTech): "The era of manual coding is over."
2,000 laid off.
$4.5 trillion in human labor shifting to machines.
notes: The screenshot includes a Resume.org survey headline surfaced in Yahoo Finance.

claim_text:
Nigeria just launched an AI-driven tracking system (March 2026) that:
1. Monitors every traveler who entered the country in the last decade.
2. Locates those who overstayed their visas.
3. Uses "data-driven performance management" for border control.
notes: The image includes a visible newspaper headline about AI to track irregular migrants and overstayed visitors.

claim_text:
Your ChatGPT Question Costs the Earth
1. Training one large AI model emits 626,000 lbs of CO2, equal to 5 cars' lifetime emissions.
2. A 20-50 question chat consumes 500ml of water (cooling data centers).
3. By 2040, ICT emissions will hit 14% of global total.
4. AI creates 5 million tons of e-waste by 2030.
notes: The screenshot shows both a Cority sustainability article and a DOAJ-indexed journal article.

claim_text:
No Consent. No Control. No Escape.
Every AI company:
1. Scrapes your data without asking.
2. Shares it with governments without telling you.
3. Profits from your identity.
4. Leaves you with zero recourse.
source_shown: No external source shown; TikTok @neonash78 overlay
notes: This slide contains no visible external publication or document.

claim_text:
AI Made Your PC More Expensive
1. DRAM prices increased 700% in one year.
2. 50% of global DRAM now goes to AI data centers (up from 32%).
3. Smartphones, laptops, and consoles cost more, or get cancelled.
source_shown: No external source shown; TikTok @neonash78 overlay
notes: No source snippet is visible on this slide.
